behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real vision-model judgment of explicitness (LLM-DEP=yes). A mocked classifier would trivially pass routing logic and hide misclassification risk (MOCK-HIDE=yes). Workflow was considered but rejected because this behavior describes one model decision plus deterministic hold/publish routing, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of real explicit images through the production moderation path using the real vision model; assert they are flagged and end in held-for-review state (never published). Include repeated runs to detect model drift-related misses.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: Correctness is robustness of real model classification under obfuscation, which is model-quality dependent (LLM-DEP=yes). Integration with stubs only tests plumbing, not detection quality (MOCK-HIDE=yes). Workflow rejected: single text classification step, not multi-step agentic dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real-model evaluation set of hate-speech variants (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, mixed unicode) through moderation and assert posts are flagged/held across variants, with thresholded pass criteria.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is module handoff and decision wiring (image result + text result -> publish/hold). This is not model-quality dependent if both checks are forced to pass via deterministic fixtures/stubs (LLM-DEP=no for this behavior). System rejected because no real OS/network/subprocess condition is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, decision layer, and persistence/queue modules in-process. Feed deterministic 'pass' outcomes for both checks and assert final state is published, with explicit assertion that no hold record or drop path is triggered.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly an end-to-end chain with multiple agentic steps in sequence (image model then text model) plus final decision (MULTI-STEP=yes). Measuring true 5s SLA requires real pipeline behavior including real model-call latency characteristics; lower levels can miss chained latency interactions. System was plausible, but behavior text says 'full moderation pipeline' across both AI checks, which matches Workflow boundary example.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end submissions through production-like pipeline with real image and text model calls enabled; measure p95/p99 completion time from submit to final decision and fail if SLA exceeds 5.0s.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: The core failure is fallback-state handling at module boundaries (model client error -> retry queue path). This can be caught by wiring real service/retry components and injecting deterministic unavailable errors (MOCK-MISS at module handoff, but REAL-INFRA not strictly required). System rejected because behavior text does not require specific real network/OS failure semantics, only correct business handling of unavailability.
behaviors.test_description: Run service + retry queue integration with model client configured to return unavailable/timeouts deterministically; assert image posts are enqueued for retry and never auto-approved.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Borderline consistency failure is driven by real model output variability; deterministic stubs cannot expose that instability (LLM-DEP=yes, MOCK-HIDE=yes). Workflow rejected because this is repeated single-step image classification consistency, not a dependent multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline images repeatedly within the target time window against the real vision model and production decision path; assert decision consistency rate meets threshold (or exact consistency if caching policy requires it).
